summary:
Ionizing radiation kills cancer cells mainly by inducing double-stranded DNA breaks, both directly and indirectly through reactive oxygen species. Cancer cells, which often have impaired DNA repair mechanisms, cannot effectively fix this damage, leading to mitochondrial dysfunction, mitotic catastrophe, and eventual cell death.  

\boxed{D}
